raw_text: Red Hat Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10 rsync: Rsync: Use-after-free vulnerability in extended attribute handling A flaw was found in rsync. When rsync is configured to handle extended attributes (using the -X or --xattrs option), a remote attacker can exploit a use-after-free vulnerability. This occurs because the receive_xattr function incorrectly processes an untrusted length value during a sorting operation, leading to memory corruption. Successful exploitation can result in a denial of service, causing the rsync process to crash, and may potentially allow for arbitrary code execution.
